---
title: Handlebars
icon: CurlyBraces
description: Build email templates with Handlebars
---

<Callout type="warn">
The Handlebars adapter is not yet implemented. The `@betternotify/handlebars` package exists but throws `NotifyRpcNotImplementedError` at runtime. This adapter will ship in a future release.
</Callout>

## Planned design

The `@betternotify/handlebars` adapter will compile Handlebars templates (as strings or file paths) and render them with the procedure's input at send time.

The planned API:

```ts
import { handlebarsTemplate } from '@betternotify/handlebars';

const welcome = rpc
  .email()
  .input(z.object({ name: z.string() }))
  .subject(({ input }) => `Welcome, ${input.name}!`)
  .template(handlebarsTemplate('./emails/welcome.hbs'));
```

Handlebars is a good fit when you have existing `.hbs` templates from another system, when non-developers need to edit templates without touching TypeScript, or when you want logic-light templates with simple `{{variable}}` interpolation.
